_meta.title: PAS 13:2017 Code of practice for safety barriers used in traffic management within workplace environments with test methods for safety barrier impact resilience
_meta.shortTitle: PAS 13:2017 Code of practice for safety barriers used in traffic management
_meta.sourceFile: British Standard - PAS_13_Code of Practice - PDF_.pdf
_meta.pageNumberingNote: Page numbers below are PDF viewer page numbers (1-indexed physical pages) — suitable for #page=N deep links. The standard's own printed page numbers (i–vi for front matter, 1–46 for body) are offset by about +8 from physical pages for the body and +2 for the front matter.
_meta.wordingRule: This standard is referenced in the app as 'PAS 13 aligned' (never 'PAS 13 compliant') — no commercial-liability claim made by this app.

definitions.definition: stopping and containing a striker without failure
failure of a barrier to arrest a striker, or structural failures within the barrier, fixture systems and structural components assessed via visual analysis
person with sufficient experience, knowledge and training to perform the task
structurally integral parts of a workplace
location within the workplace environment where a pedestrian may move across a vehicle area
movement occurring in barriers during impact
area identified to allow barrier deformation without impinging on pedestrian safe zone
naturally-preferred route a pedestrian takes across a workplace environment
first and last posts in a run of barriers
technically-detailed graphic depiction of a barrier
"off-highway" work areas that are not on the road
system that anchors or fixes a barrier to the floor
graphic representation of a workplace showing pedestrian and vehicle zones, structures and equipment
specialist camera for photographing high-velocity impacts
specific angle of approach between a vehicle and barrier
energy an object possesses due to its motion (measured in joules (J) or Newton metres (Nm))
device to measure and display force
area within a workplace where items are placed on or taken for the purpose of transfer
hand-powered machinery in a workplace
post connecting two aligned rails
person moving and located within and around the workplace environment
defined zone between pedestrian walkway and traffic route
area in workplace where traffic is not permitted, such as walkways or work areas
all people within the workplace
component, generally horizontal in alignment, that is connected between two or more posts
elevated edge that can be used for traffic or pedestrian guidance
part designed to fail in a safe and controlled manner
workplace protection item (examples include hand rails, pedestrian barriers, guard rails, traffic barriers, crash barriers, vehicle barriers, racking barriers and bollards)
protection item placed around a test barrier to capture flying debris following striker impact
accurate and approved calibrated weighing implement
separation of vehicles and pedestrians within a workplace
object or vehicle that impacts a barrier
integral support or framework of a building
visible position marked for impact on a test barrier
location where performance measurements are made on test barriers
safety barrier used specifically for test purposes
procedures for the safe guidance of vehicles and protection of personnel within a workplace environment
moving equipment within the workplace environment
object's speed, measured in m/s (metres per second), and its moving direction
route within the work environment specifically for the movement of pedestrians
general area within a workplace where workers are present
general place of work
